codeReview.summary: The reviewed commit only adds a verification screenshot and does not adjust warning behavior. The warning text does include 'cached', but visibility logic still misses valid non-empty states.

issues.description: When `amount` is prefilled from URL params (`if (params.get('amount')) quoteAmountInput.value = params.get('amount');`), the warning visibility is not recomputed, so a non-empty amount can render with the warning hidden. This violates the expected behavior that warning is visible when amount is non-empty.

The input handler only shows the warning when `parseFloat(value) > 0`, not for all non-empty values. Feature requirements specify visibility by non-empty amount state, so the current condition is narrower than required.

sharedStateObservations.observation: `frontend-worker` procedure says to always read `src/server.py`, `nginx.conf`, `.factory/library/tokenlist-format.md`, and `.factory/library/architecture.md` before implementing, but this session only read static UI files and still reported `followedProcedure: true`. The skill steps appear stricter than actual practice for small UI-only verification tasks.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/frontend-worker/SKILL.md step 2 lists required reads; transcript skeleton for workerSessionId 8c9430af-bb37-4158-a1ee-a97edafaacbd shows reads of static/js/app.js, static/index.html, static/css/style.css, and .factory/services.yaml only.

summary: Review failed: commit 81afbc6 only adds evidence, while warning visibility logic still misses non-empty cases (notably URL-prefilled amount).
